items.claim: Cole has a tendency to abandon technical striking to swing wildly with his head dipped.
items.quote: Sometimes out of nowhere, he'll go from looking super clean and super technical to just dipping his head and just swinging wild.

items.claim: Cole possesses a very good sprawl and is difficult to take down.
items.quote: Very good sprawl. Really really hard to get get into the hips on.

items.claim: Austin Bashi's wrestling and grappling have become his superior skill sets over his Muay Thai base.
items.quote: His wrestling and his grappling have become his better skill sets over the over the years.

items.claim: Austin Bashi is highly effective at taking the back and maintaining control when takedowns are initiated.
items.quote: He advances position often looking to take the back. Once he gets to the back, he's a real big problem.

items.claim: Jose Delgado excels at maintaining range while applying forward pressure.
items.quote: He's got good forward pressure and he maintains range while doing so. Not many people can push forward and also keep you at the end of their punches.

items.claim: Jose Delgado relies heavily on head movement, which results in him absorbing clean shots when he is caught.
items.quote: But the problem is he does rely on his head movement... when it does hit, you know, if that's what you're relying on... when it lands on him, he's just eating it, right?

items.claim: Jose Delgado possesses more power than Austin Bashi.
items.quote: he's got it. He's going to have the probably the more power in this fight.
